event: tried to lose weight without success
underwent a Da Vinci-assisted, laparoscopic, one-stage, single-anastomosis DS procedure
terminal ileum was identified and ran 250 cm proximally up the ileum
length of the common channel was defined
marked the site of our duodeno-ileal anastomosis
greater omentum was taken from the greater curvature distally 3 cm from the pylorus
a window was created for transection of the duodenum
sleeve gastrectomy was done loosely over a 34 French bougie-sized Edlich tube
duodenum was transected 3 cm from the pylorus
duodenal stump was oversewn with a running absorbable suture
two-layer hand sewn anastomosis between the duodenum and the ileum was created at the 250 cm mark
anastomosis was tested with methylene blue and air and it showed no leakage
a drain was placed in left upper quadrant
upper gastrointestinal study on POD 2 was negative for a leak
computed tomography of the abdomen showing free fluid suggestive of a leak or bleeding
taken to the operating room for diagnostic laparoscopy
active bleeding source was not identifiable
complete inspection of the gastroileostomy anastomosis was performed without significant findings
no leakage seen with a methylene blue test
attempt to explore the duodenal stump was unsuccessful due to retraction and inflammation
three large 19F Blake drains were placed
transfused with 2 units of packed red blood cells intra-operatively
abdominal drain had increased sanguinous output
taken back to the operating room for exploratory laparotomy
presumptive diagnosis of duodenal stump blowout
laparotomy through a generous right subcostal incision
large amount of hemoperitoneum was encountered
duodenal stump had total stump blow and the staple line was completely undone
Malecot tube was placed in the duodenum and over-sewn with a purse-string suture
abdomen was closed and drains were placed
received 5 units of packed red blood cells intra-operatively
received 4 units of fresh frozen plasma intra-operatively
hemorrhagic fluid collections seen on CT scan
acute thrombosis of the left internal jugular vein at a previous central catheter site
acute thrombosis of the subclavian vein at a previous central catheter site
adhesions of the biliopancreatic limb to the anterior abdominal wall
computed tomography showing the inflammation around the duodenal stump site
discharged to her home on POD 17
home health care for dressing changes
